messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, EventType_reg, ImageLoaded, Signed, PipeName, QueryName, TargetFilename, TamperingType. Identify lateral movement tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Sysmon — WMI Lateral Execution.
Source: APP-41 → Target: SRV-94
  Method: Win32_Process.Create via ManagementScope
  ParentProcess: WmiPrvSe.exe
  ChildProcess: powershell.exe
  CommandLine: powershell.exe -enc 4f4b
  network_logon_event_4624=YES (type 3, from APP-41)
  rpc_connection_to_port_135=YES

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  SCCM, Intune, and IT management tools use WMI for inventory and configuration, but these run from service accounts on scheduled cycles. WmiPrvSe.exe spawning powershell.exe directly without a prior scheduled task or maintenance event is not legitimate admin behavior.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  ParentProcess=WmiPrvSe.exe spawning powershell.exe: process launched via Win32_Process.Create (not a scheduled or persistent event). Event 4624 network logon (type 3) from APP-41 immediately before process creation. Payload='powershell.exe -enc 4f4b' (PowerShell/temp binary from WMI context).
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Source APP-41 executed arbitrary command on SRV-94 via WMI. Execution appears as WmiPrvSe.exe child process — evades process-based blocking. Network channel: RPC port 135 + dynamic ports (no SMB 445 needed).
[CONCLUSION] WMI remote code execution via Win32_Process.Create confirmed.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1047 (Windows Management Instrumentation). Isolate target, check WmiPrvSe.exe process tree, audit WMI subscriptions.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Sysmon — WMI Query from Management Service.
  Source: svc-sccm (service account)
  WMI_class: Win32_LogicalDisk  operation=SELECT (read-only)
  schedule=hourly inventory  no_process_creation=YES
  parent=SCCMAgent.exe

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  SCCM agent WMI inventory query — read-only, service account, scheduled, no process creation.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  class=Win32_LogicalDisk (read-only). no Win32_Process.Create. service account. SCCMAgent.exe parent.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized SCCM inventory via WMI — no lateral execution.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized SCCM WMI inventory. No action.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1047 — AUTHORIZED IT MANAGEMENT. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
